name: Almgren, R. and Chriss, N. (2000) Optimal Execution of Portfolio
snippet: We present a trading execution model that describes the behaviour of a big trader and of a multitude of retail traders operating on the shares of a risky asset.

name: Understanding the Almgren-Chriss Model for Optimal Trade Execution
snippet:
One of the most elegant results of the Almgren-Chriss model is the closed-form solution for the optimal trading trajectory.
The Almgren-Chriss model, developed by Robert Almgren and Neil Chriss in 2000, provides a mathematical framework to solve this problem. It has become an important model of algorithmic trading strategies used by investment banks, hedge funds, and asset managers worldwide.

name: Understanding the Almgren-Chriss Model for Optimal Portfolio Execution
snippet: The Almgren-Chriss model allows traders to systematically determine the optimal execution strategy, quantitative methods in modern finance.

name: Solving the Almgren Chris Model - Dean Markwick
snippet:
The Almgren Chris model from Optimal Execution of Portfolio Transactions is the most well known optimal execution model and provides the foundational math.
We’ve gone through the full problem set-up and show how the TWAP minimises expected costs for a risk-neutral investor and how an exponential execution schedule minimises cost for a risk-sensitive investor.
